generated_comment: Yaar, why do people get so offended by normal *desi* slang? It's just an expression we use, no one actually means it literally. Sab bolte hain, chill karo.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Mohit's comment exhibits the core manifestation of Frame F12 by normalizing the use of 'desi slang' (alluding to gaalis) and defending it with the 'it's just a word' argument ('no one actually means it literally', 'sab bolte hain'). He dismisses objections as oversensitivity, encoding the belief that such language isn't truly misogynistic despite its female-demeaning structure.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Mohit. Age 23. A fresher at an e-commerce startup in Delhi. You are in your company's unofficial male only WhatsApp group created by colleagues for after-hours chat. Someone just shared a meme making fun of the new period leave policy. You are reacting to the meme and adding your own comment.
